description: Gyarados's Water/Flying typing covers Abomasnow's double fire weakness and fighting weakness. Creator explicitly calls Gyarados 'a good partner' for Abomasnow and later refers to this as the 'Gyarados Abomasnow core.'

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: SHADOW ABOMASNOW LOCKS DOWN WATER-TYPES in the Jungle Cup!!

description: Double flyer core that functions as an ABA bracket — despite both being flyers, Corviknight's Steel typing and Altaria's Dragon typing give them very different resistances and coverage, making them hard to counter with a single answer.

sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion
sources.videoTitle: Quagsire is a SPAMMY MENACE Once Again!! | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Lanturn and Altaria pair well with Togekiss — the creator explicitly states these two 'pair pretty well with it', covering Togekiss's weaknesses in the Jungle Cup meta.

sources.author: ItsAXN
sources.videoTitle: Togekiss Has The PERFECT Coverage For GBL [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Altaria covers Quagsire's grass weakness, while Water Gun Quagsire covers Altaria's ice weakness (Piloswine, a main ice threat in the meta, is weak to water). Together they form the back pair that relies on alignment after Talonflame forces a switch.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: UNEXPECTED WATER DAMAGE | Water Gun QUAGSIRE Jungle Cup Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Drapion pressures shields early with spammy Aqua Tails and Crunches, saving everything for Charizard to sweep. The entire team strategy revolves around Drapion burning the opponent's shields so Charizard can close with Ember energy and unshielded Blast Burns/Air Cutters.

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: Doing MASSIVE DAMAGE & CLIMBING with this TRIPLE SHADOW TEAM in the Jungle Cup!!

description: Malamar bridges Charizard in by cleaning up after Drapion falls, and enables 'combo plays' where Malamar banks Superpower energy to finish off what Charizard weakened — or vice versa, Charizard farms down and leaves targets in Malamar's Psywave/Superpower range.

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: Doing MASSIVE DAMAGE & CLIMBING with this TRIPLE SHADOW TEAM in the Jungle Cup!!

description: Gyarados 'pairs very well with Corviknight' — Corviknight walls threats like Garchomp that could trouble Gyarados, while Gyarados handles water/fire types that threaten Corviknight.

description: Corviknight plus Quagsire is explicitly called 'a pretty good core to have switch with' — they cover each other's weaknesses and provide solid backline support behind the Gyarados safe swap.

sources.author: ItsAXN
sources.videoTitle: BUFFED Twister Gyarados Is A MENACE In Jungle Cup [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Swampert's only weakness is grass, and Corviknight double resists grass — a classic complementary core that covers each other's key vulnerability.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: SWAMPERT META AGAIN  | Jungle Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Double shadow ground core — creator explicitly highlights this as a 'double ground comp' with two shadows in the back. Both spam Sand Tomb for defense debuffs, and their combined ground/flying typing provides broad coverage behind the Gyarados lead.

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: Gyarados puts on INSANE PRESSURE with BUFFED WATERFALL in the Jungle Cup!!

description: Forretress covers Togekiss's poison weakness with its Steel typing, while Togekiss carries Aura Sphere to handle steels and covers fighting threats with its Fairy/Flying typing. Creator explicitly highlights this defensive synergy.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: TOGEKISS is a TOP TIER Jungle Cup Pick | Jungle Cup Team | Pokémon GO

description: Qwilfish acts as the safe swap that absorbs damage and builds energy, while Gourgeist closes in the back. Qwilfish handles the threats Gourgeist struggles with (like Togekiss and fighters), and when Qwilfish does its job softening opponents, Gourgeist can clean up with shield advantage.

description: Gyarados 'pairs very well with Quagsire' — Quagsire's Water Gun pressure complements Gyarados's Dragon Breath/Aqua Tail spam, and they share water-type synergy while covering different threats.

sources.videoTitle: BUFFED Twister Gyarados Is A MENACE In Jungle Cup [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Shadow Gyarados and Scrafty form the backbone of this team — Scrafty handles the normal/ice/rock/steel types that dominate the Jungle Cup top 20, while Gyarados pressures the fire and ground types that threaten Scrafty.

sources.videoTitle: GYARADOS goes WILD  | Jungle Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Both shadow Pokemon apply massive fast move pressure (Sucker Punch + Thunder Fang) compounded by defense-dropping charge moves (Poison Fang + Psych Fangs). Creator notes they cover each other's matchups — Mightyena handles what Steelix can't and vice versa, with Steelix walling anything not super effective.

sources.videoTitle: The MOST TOXIC TEAM wins you battles in SECONDS in the Jungle Cup!

description: Mightyena's Poison Fang drops the opponent's defense, enabling Wigglytuff's Charm to farm down debuffed opponents. Creator explicitly highlights this sequence: 'The debuff I'm looking for because now they would be even in a position where charm could farm them all the way down.'

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The MOST TOXIC TEAM wins you battles in SECONDS in the Jungle Cup!

description: Opponent core identified by the creator as very common in the Jungle Cup meta — Togekiss and Quagsire cover each other's weaknesses, with Quagsire countering Togekiss's vulnerabilities.

sources.videoTitle: INSANE 13-2 RUN with SHADOW CAMERUPT in the Jungle Cup!!

description: Scrafty is weak to fighting and fairy; Talonflame as fire/flying resists both. Talonflame's water and electric weaknesses are handled reasonably well by Scrafty's bulk and coverage. Creator explicitly highlights this defensive synergy.

description: Swalot covers Toucannon's weaknesses while Toucannon covers Swalot's Ground weakness as a flyer. Both are spammy and hard-hitting enough to sweep together, and Swalot adds bulk to complement Toucannon's attack-heavy stats.
